topic: Amazon EC2 Instance Resource Allocation for Neptune

  entities:
  
    Amazon EC2|Service
    Neptune|Service
    Amazon EC2 instances|Product
    Amazon EC2 instance type|Product
    Amazon EC2 instance size|Product
    vCPUs|Product
    system memory|Product
    primary storage|Product
    DB instances|Product
    cluster|Product
    external primary storage|Product
    compute|Product
    storage capacity|Product
    instance families|Product
    query execution threads|Product
    vCPU|Product
    instance size|Product
    Neptune DB instance|Product
    DFE query engine|Software
    SPARQL queries|Software
    openCypher queries|Software
    Gremlin read queries|Software
    DB cluster|Product
    query queue|Product
    /gremlin/status API|Software
    /oc/status API|Software
    /sparql/status API|Software
    MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue CloudWatch metric|Product
    CloudWatch|Service
    buffer pool cache|Product
    buffer-pool cache|Product
    graph|Product
    instances|Product
    buffer cache hit and miss metrics|Product
    cache hit rate|Product
    buffer pool|Product
    engine|Product
    underlying storage volume|Product
    query execution threads|Product
    operating system|Software
    dynamic pool|Product
    threads|Product
    8xl instance type|Product
    OutOfMemoryException|Software
    OOM|Software
    thread|Product
    instance|Product

  proposition: Amazon EC2 is a cloud computing service.
    
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    Amazon EC2|SERVICE_TYPE|cloud computing service
      
  proposition: Neptune is a database service that uses Amazon EC2 instances.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Neptune|USES|Amazon EC2 instances
  
    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SERVICE_TYPE|database service
    
  proposition: Each Amazon EC2 instance type offers a defined amount of compute measured in vCPUs.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Amazon EC2 instance type|OFFERS|compute
    compute|MEASURED_IN|vCPUs
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Each Amazon EC2 instance size offers a defined amount of compute measured in vCPUs.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Amazon EC2 instance size|OFFERS|compute
    compute|MEASURED_IN|vCPUs
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Each Amazon EC2 instance type offers a defined amount of system memory.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Amazon EC2 instance type|OFFERS|system memory
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Each Amazon EC2 instance size offers a defined amount of system memory.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Amazon EC2 instance size|OFFERS|system memory
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: The primary storage for Neptune is external to the DB instances in a cluster.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    primary storage|FOR|Neptune
    DB instances|PART_OF|cluster
  
    entity-attributes:
    primary storage|LOCATION|external to the DB instances in a cluster
    
  proposition: The external primary storage lets compute and storage capacity scale independently of each other.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    external primary storage|ENABLES|compute
    external primary storage|ENABLES|storage capacity
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: In all instance families, vCPU resources are allocated to support two query execution threads per vCPU.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    instance families|CONTAINS|vCPU
    vCPU|SUPPORTS|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    query execution threads|QUANTITY_PER_VCPU|two
    
  proposition: The support for two query execution threads per vCPU is dictated by the instance size.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    instance size|DICTATES|query execution threads
    query execution threads|SUPPORTED_BY|vCPU
  
    entity-attributes:
    query execution threads|QUANTITY_PER_VCPU|two
    
  proposition: When determining the proper size of a given Neptune DB instance, you need to consider the possible concurrency of your application.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Neptune DB instance|HAS|size
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: When determining the proper size of a given Neptune DB instance, you need to consider the average latency of your queries.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Neptune DB instance|HAS|size
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: You can estimate the number of vCPUs needed using the formula vCPUs=(latencyxconcurrency)/2.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
  
    entity-attributes:
    vCPUs|FORMULA|vCPUs=(latencyxconcurrency)/2
    
  proposition: In the vCPU estimation formula, latency is measured as the average query latency in seconds.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    vCPU|HAS|formula
  
    entity-attributes:
    latency|MEASURED_AS|average query latency in seconds
    
  proposition: In the vCPU estimation formula, concurrency is measured as the target number of queries per second.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    vCPU|HAS|formula
  
    entity-attributes:
    concurrency|MEASURED_AS|target number of queries per second
    
  proposition: SPARQL queries can use more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances when using the DFE query engine.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    SPARQL queries|USES|DFE query engine
    SPARQL queries|USES|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    SPARQL queries|THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances
    
  proposition: openCypher queries can use more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances when using the DFE query engine.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    openCypher queries|USES|DFE query engine
    openCypher queries|USES|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    openCypher queries|THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances
    
  proposition: Gremlin read queries can use more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances when using the DFE query engine.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    Gremlin read queries|USES|DFE query engine
    Gremlin read queries|USES|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    Gremlin read queries|THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances
    
  proposition: DFE query engine is a query processing engine used by Neptune.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    DFE query engine|USED_BY|Neptune
  
    entity-attributes:
    DFE query engine|TYPE|query processing engine
    
  proposition: When initially sizing your DB cluster, start with the assumption that each query will consume a single execution thread per execution.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    DB cluster|HAS|size
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: You should scale up if you observe back pressure into query queue.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Back pressure into query queue can be observed by using the /gremlin/status API.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    /gremlin/status API|OBSERVES|query queue
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Back pressure into query queue can be observed by using the /oc/status API.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    /oc/status API|OBSERVES|query queue
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Back pressure into query queue can be observed by using the /sparql/status API.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    /sparql/status API|OBSERVES|query queue
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Back pressure into query queue can be observed using the MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue CloudWatch metric.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue CloudWatch metric|OBSERVES|query queue
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: CloudWatch is a monitoring service that provides metrics.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    CloudWatch|PROVIDES|metrics
  
    entity-attributes:
    CloudWatch|SERVICE_TYPE|monitoring service
    
  proposition: System memory on each instance is divided into two primary allocations: buffer pool cache and query execution thread memory.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    system memory|PART_OF|instances
    system memory|DIVIDED_INTO|buffer pool cache
    system memory|DIVIDED_INTO|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    system memory|ALLOCATION_COUNT|two primary allocations
    
  proposition: Approximately two thirds of the available memory in an instance is allocated for buffer-pool cache.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    instances|CONTAINS|memory
    buffer-pool cache|ALLOCATED_FROM|memory
  
    entity-attributes:
    buffer-pool cache|MEMORY_ALLOCATION|approximately two thirds
    
  proposition: Buffer-pool cache is used to cache the most recently used components of the graph for faster access on queries.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    buffer-pool cache|CACHES|graph
  
    entity-attributes:
    buffer-pool cache|PURPOSE|cache the most recently used components of the graph for faster access on queries
    
  proposition: Buffer-pool cache provides faster access on queries that repeatedly access those components.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    buffer-pool cache|PROVIDES|access
  
    entity-attributes:
    buffer-pool cache|BENEFIT|faster access on queries that repeatedly access those components
    
  proposition: Instances with a larger amount of system memory have larger buffer pool caches.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    instances|HAS|system memory
    instances|HAS|buffer pool cache
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Larger buffer pool caches can store more of the graph locally.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    buffer pool cache|STORES|graph
  
    entity-attributes:
    buffer pool cache|STORAGE_CAPACITY|more of the graph locally
    
  proposition: A user can tune for the appropriate amount of buffer-pool cache by monitoring the buffer cache hit and miss metrics available in CloudWatch.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    buffer cache hit and miss metrics|AVAILABLE_IN|CloudWatch
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: You may want to increase the size of your instance if the cache hit rate drops below 99.9% for a consistent period of time.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    instances|HAS|size
    cache hit rate|BELONGS_TO|instances
  
    entity-attributes:
    cache hit rate|THRESHOLD|99.9%
    
  proposition: A cache hit rate below 99.9% for a consistent period suggests that the buffer pool is not big enough.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    cache hit rate|INDICATES|buffer pool
  
    entity-attributes:
    cache hit rate|THRESHOLD|99.9%
    
  proposition: When the buffer pool is not big enough, the engine is having to fetch data from the underlying storage volume more often than is efficient.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    buffer pool|AFFECTS|engine
    engine|FETCHES_FROM|underlying storage volume
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: The remaining third of system memory is distributed evenly across query execution threads.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    system memory|DISTRIBUTED_TO|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    system memory|DISTRIBUTION_AMOUNT|remaining third
    system memory|DISTRIBUTION_METHOD|evenly
    
  proposition: Some memory remains for the operating system after distributing memory to query execution threads.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    memory|REMAINS_FOR|operating system
    memory|DISTRIBUTED_TO|query execution threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: Some memory remains for a small dynamic pool for threads to use as needed.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    memory|REMAINS_FOR|dynamic pool
    dynamic pool|USED_BY|threads
  
    entity-attributes:
    dynamic pool|SIZE|small
    
  proposition: The memory available for each thread increases slightly from one instance size to the next up to an 8xl instance type.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    memory|AVAILABLE_FOR|thread
    thread|PART_OF|instance size
    instance size|SCALES_TO|8xl instance type
  
    entity-attributes:
    memory|INCREASE_PATTERN|slightly from one instance size to the next
    
  proposition: At 8xl instance type size, the memory allocated per thread reaches a maximum.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    8xl instance type|ALLOCATES|memory
    memory|ALLOCATED_TO|thread
  
    entity-attributes:
    memory|ALLOCATION_STATUS|maximum
    
  proposition: The time to add more thread memory is when you encounter an OutOfMemoryException.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    thread|HAS|memory
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: OutOfMemoryException is abbreviated as OOM.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
  
    entity-attributes:
    OutOfMemoryException|ABBREVIATION|OOM
    
  proposition: OOM exceptions occur when one thread needs more than the maximum memory allocated to it.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    OOM|OCCURS_WITH|thread
    thread|ALLOCATED|memory
  
    entity-attributes:
    OOM|CAUSE|when one thread needs more than the maximum memory allocated to it
    
  proposition: OOM exceptions are not the same as the entire instance running out of memory.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    OOM|DIFFERENT_FROM|instance
    instance|HAS|memory
  
    entity-attributes:
